goal: Predict bulk_modulus, density, tg for PSFO (*Oc1ccc(C(C)(C)c2ccc(Oc3ccc(S(=O)(=O)c4ccc(*)cc4)cc3)cc2)cc1)
assumptions: SMILES is BPA-polysulfone (Udel/PSU): isopropylidene C(C)(C) bridge between two aryls + aryl-ether-sulfone linkage. Member resolved as PSU (not PES/PPSU which lack the BPA bridge), so experimental_tg_K=463 K and exp_density=1.24 g/cm3 (PSFO class dict lists PSU:463, PES:493, PPSU:493).
PCFF FF transferability to aryl-SO2-aryl + aryl-ether validated by analogy (Hayashi2022, Afzal2021); no PSU-specific PCFF MD Tg/density paper, hence class confidence=medium.

RESOURCES (orchestrator-owned; no schema field, recorded here per policy): gpu_ids="0,1,2,3", mpi=4. User resolved the round-1 budget-vs-policy conflict (run_log R-00) by allocating all 4 GPUs with MPI=4 (~4x throughput vs the original GPU=3/MPI=1), which makes a policy-compliant DP=20 feasible within the 48h budget. The orchestrator injects these into worker prompts.

uncertainties.name: ff_transferability
uncertainties.detail: PCFF has parameter coverage for aryl-SO2-aryl and aryl-ether via Class II cross terms but no PSU-specific MD Tg/density/K validation paper; mitigated by Hayashi2022/Afzal2021 analogy. With DP raised to the policy-compliant 20 (round-2 resolution), short-chain finite-size bias is no longer the leading residual; FF transferability to the polar sulfone+ether backbone is now the dominant uncertainty. literature_anchor (search_ff_literature, ROADMAP E2) recorded as PLANNED, not executed.

evidence.claim: PCFF via EMC. Sun1994 (CFF93/PCFF) is the FF origin; no class-specific PCFF MD validation paper available yet (see Track G in ROADMAP.md).

alternatives.known_error: Generic FF; lacks Class II cross terms important for rigid aromatic backbone torsions; expected Tg/modulus bias larger than PCFF for stiff aryl-sulfone chains.
alternatives.rejected_because: PCFF covers aryl-SO2-aryl and aryl-ether via cross terms; preferred builder EMC emits PCFF directly.

alternatives.known_error: OPLS-AA sulfone (-SO2-) parameters less validated for polymer condensed phase; no PSU MD precedent.
alternatives.rejected_because: No advantage over PCFF for this backbone; PCFF is class default with supporting citations.

evidence.claim: Sulfone -SO2- carries very large partial charges (S ~+1.3 e, O ~-0.55 e) and ether O adds polarity; bond-increment/Gasteiger charges underrepresent these, so QM-derived RESP charges are warranted for accurate electrostatics throughout the polar backbone.
RESP is more costly than FF-embedded charges but the cost is one-time per repeat unit and negligible vs the equilibration/Tg budget.

alternatives.known_error: Underrepresents large sulfone partial charges; biases density/CED for strongly polar backbone.
alternatives.rejected_because: Sulfone+ether polarity demands QM charges; RESP chosen despite PCFF normally pairing with bond-increment.

evidence.claim: Backbone contains S and O heteroatoms with |q|>>0.1 e (sulfone S ~+1.3 e); per electrostatics_decision_guide such backbones MUST use PPPM long-range Coulomb. Neglecting it biases density/Tg 5-20%.
PPPM adds modest GPU cost vs lj_cut but is mandatory here; cutoff_A=12.0 sets the real-space split.

alternatives.rejected_because: Disallowed by policy for heteroatom/|q|>0.1e backbones.

evidence.claim: DP=20 satisfies the policy HARD floor 'DP>=20 (Fox-Flory plateau) for Tg targets'. Per-chain MW ~8,850 g/mol (442.5 g/mol repeat unit x 20), system total ~70,800 g/mol over nchain=8, ~8,640 atoms. Round-2 resolution: the original DP=15 (per-chain MW ~5,304, below the Fox-Flory/MW-convergence floor) is dropped; the 4-GPU/MPI=4 allocation makes DP=20 feasible within the 48h budget.

bulk_modulus target: glassy K is computed via the Born/NVT elastic tensor at 300 K (<< Tg=463 K), a LOCAL elastic response governed by segmental packing and cohesive energy density, not by the entanglement network. The per-chain MW requirement for K is therefore met with margin at DP=20 (~8,850 g/mol, ~1 entanglement MW for polysulfone and well above the DP=15 below-threshold case); no per-class Me is tabulated in polymer_rules.json, so K validity rests on the segmental-elastic physics rather than network entanglement, which is robust at this chain length.

Self-imaging check: at final density 1.24 g/cm3 the cubic cell edge L ~45.6 A (L/2 ~22.8 A) and at initial 0.62 g/cm3 L ~57.5 A; both give L/2 >> cutoff_A=12.0, so nchain=8 (the PSFO class default) gives a cell large enough to avoid minimum-image self-interaction. No nchain increase needed.
PSU experimental RT density ~1.24 g/cm3; initial density 0.62 g/cm3 (~0.5x) for EMC packing.

alternatives.known_error: Per-chain MW ~5,304 g/mol is below the Fox-Flory DP>=20 floor; rigid aromatic backbone at DP=15 overestimates Tg by ~5-15% beyond standard MD bias and biases K high.
alternatives.rejected_because: Violates the policy HARD require DP>=20 for Tg targets (critic round-1 finding); the 4-GPU/MPI=4 allocation removes the budget rationale that motivated it.

alternatives.known_error: Further above the MW threshold but ~1.5-2x the atom count of DP=20/nchain=8; exceeds the 48h budget even with 4 GPUs.
alternatives.rejected_because: DP=20 already clears the Fox-Flory floor and the self-imaging check; extra chain length wastes GPU budget for no required accuracy gain.

evidence.claim: PSU experimental Tg=463 K >> 300 K so the workflow phase at 300 K is glassy; glassy bulk modulus uses rate-free Born+NVT (full elastic tensor) per policy, with uniaxial deform as fallback only (deform overestimates K 20-50%).
Experimental K reference 3.0-5.0 GPa (and E ~2.5-2.6 GPa, Mark2007) anchors the analyze-bm comparison.

alternatives.known_error: Both-phase candidate but no bm_pressures_atm defined for this class; Born is the validated glassy path.
alternatives.rejected_because: Class is glassy at 300 K and Born is the preferred glassy method.

alternatives.known_error: Cross-check only; noisy for stiff glass.
alternatives.rejected_because: Used only as cross-check, not primary.

critique.round1_resolution: Round-1 REVISE finding addressed: D-04 raised from DP=15 to the policy-compliant DP=20 (above the Fox-Flory plateau floor for the Tg target; per-chain MW ~8,850 g/mol with K validity resting on glassy segmental-elastic physics). nchain held at 8 after a self-imaging check (L/2 ~22.8 A >> cutoff 12.0 A at final density). GPU-budget-vs-policy conflict resolved by the user via 4-GPU/MPI=4 allocation (run_log R-00). Dominant uncertainty changed from short_chain_bias_DP15 (resolved) to ff_transferability.
